data.narrative:
Cailabs, a Series C-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised €57M+, is valued within a comparable universe of five private peers at the same stage and sector: Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Isar Aerospace. Public comparables in Space & Aerospace trade at a median P/S of 45.3x across six tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL), though this method remains inapplicable given the absence of disclosed revenue for private company analysis.

The peer-median method anchors valuation to the median observed across the five Series C Space & Aerospace comparables, which stands at $1.5B. The peer distribution ranges from $1.0B (Isar Aerospace) to $2.5B (Axiom Space), establishing a floor and ceiling against which Cailabs can be positioned. This method represents the sole quantitatively applicable triangulation approach given available data constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, with a central estimate of $1.5B. This range reflects the interquartile positioning within the peer set and accounts for typical stage-based and operational differentiation. Key uncertainties include Cailabs' specific revenue trajectory relative to peers, unit economics in its target market segment, and relative technology maturity—variables not addressable through comparable company analysis alone and requiring deeper operational due diligence to refine the estimate.
